title: Translation Units, Declarations, Diagnostics, and Linking · C Memory Workbench
summary: A guided implementation pair receives a clean input trace and acceptance examples, then builds the first working warning-clean multi-file interface dossier while narrating each size, lifetime, ownership, and cleanup decision. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of warning-clean multi-file interface dossier while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.
Distinguish declarations, tentative and full definitions, linkage, compatible types, and one-definition responsibilities.
Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Preprocessing Boundaries
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop native-systems team handles memory case 3675. Produce bounded C evidence to model translation and diagnostic gates before execution; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Predict macro expansion, conditional compilation, include guards, and header ownership before translation.

What must be established before implementation?

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Link Symbol Forensics case, omit size, bounds, lifetime, owner, allocation failure, cleanup, invariant output, and native transfer gates, then call the browser result production-safe.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Link Symbol Forensics condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose duplicate, missing, hidden, weak, and mismatched symbols from object and linker evidence.
